commands.author: clawSean

target.title: fix(tts): allow trusted local media in message_tool_only mode
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#83636.

Trusted local TTS media generated by tools can be dropped in `message_tool_only` room-event flows because the media was attached to the message-tool source-reply transcript mirror, then final dispatch suppressed that mirror to avoid duplicate source replies.

## The Fix

- Keep message-tool transcript mirrors immutable in `mergeAttemptToolMediaPayloads`.
- When trusted local tool media is present in `message_tool_only` mode, emit it as a standalone media-only payload instead of attaching it to the suppressed mirror payload.
- Allow only trusted local payloads with actual media to bypass `message_tool_only` source-reply suppression in final dispatch.
- Keep ordinary final text and untrusted media suppressed in ambient room-event contexts.

## Changed Files

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.ts`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: trusted local TTS media was queued by the tool path but could be attached to a `message_tool_only` source-reply mirror payload that final dispatch suppresses in ambient room-event contexts.
- Real environment tested: live OpenClaw Telegram channel path was previously verified in the same private Telegram topic by generating a local TTS voice note and sending it as a Telegram voice message. This proves the generated audio and Telegram voice-note delivery path are functional in the target environment.
- Evidence from live environment: Telegram voice note delivered to the channel topic (message 2979): https://t.me/c/1003778833824/2397/2979 . The topic is private, so the link is not publicly inspectable without membership.
- Exact commands/tests run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
  - `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- Observed result after this patch: focused helper and dispatch regression tests pass. The tests now cover the actual delivery-shape contract: trusted local TTS media is split into a standalone media-only payload, that payload is deliverable under `message_tool_only` room-event suppression, and untrusted media remains suppressed.
- What was not tested: a live dynamic `tts` tool auto-delivery run on this PR branch. That would require deploying/restarting a live OpenClaw gateway or Mantis/maintainer-run Telegram Desktop proof. The prior live Telegram proof covers the generated-media/channel-delivery prerequisites, not the full patched auto-delivery path.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` — passed, 2 shards, 216 tests.
- `git diff --check` — passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...` — passed.
- `pnpm exec oxlint ...` — passed.
- `pnpm check:changed` — attempted, but local Crabbox delegation failed before code checks with `selected binary failed basic --version/--help sanity checks`.

## Notes

This keeps the scope narrow: trusted local media gets a deliverable media-only path, while ordinary source replies and untrusted media stay suppressed under `message_tool_only`.
